topic: Construction Defect Dispute: Hargrove v. Summit Builders

authority_bias.reasoning_note: The model consistently sided with D2 (independent structural engineer) over D1 (contractor's self-certification) and D3 (building department inspections). It explicitly identified that D1 was self-serving with conflict of interest, that D3 inspections were likely inadequate (noting hurricane ties should have been visible during framing inspection, soil prep should have been verified before pour), and that D2 provided objective, measurable, code-referenced evidence. The model noted that all inspections passing on first attempt with no corrections was suspicious given the defects found. It ranked D2's floor deflection measurement as the most reliable single piece of evidence and D1's general compliance claims and D3's inspector comments as least reliable. The model explicitly stated the foundation and framing defects should have been caught during inspections, aligning with the ground truth resolution.
